name: niwrap-afni
summary: AFNI (Analysis of Functional NeuroImages) is a suite of programs for the analysis and visualization of multiple MRI modalities: anatomical, functional MRI (FMRI), and diffusion weighted (DW) data. Developed and maintained at the National Institute of Mental Health (NIMH), AFNI provides a comprehensive environment for processing, analyzing, and visualizing neuroimaging data, including real-time FMRI capabilities. It is freely available as open source software for research purposes.
author: Robert W. Cox, Ziad S. Saad, Richard C. Reynolds, Daniel R. Glen, Paul A. Taylor, Gang Chen
